statement.focus_question: Let $z_1,\ldots,z_n\in \mathbb{C}$ with $\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert\leq 2$ for all $i,j$, and\[\Delta(z_1,\ldots,z_n)=\prod_{i\neq j}\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert.\]What is the maximum possible value of $\Delta$?
statement.normalized: Let $z_1,\ldots,z_n\in \mathbb{C}$ with $\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert\leq 2$ for all $i,j$, and\[\Delta(z_1,\ldots,z_n)=\prod_{i\neq j}\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert.\]What is the maximum possible value of $\Delta$? Is it maximised by taking the $z_i$ to be the vertices of a regular polygon?
statement.original: Let $z_1,\ldots,z_n\in \mathbb{C}$ with $\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert\leq 2$ for all $i,j$, and\[\Delta(z_1,\ldots,z_n)=\prod_{i\neq j}\lvert z_i-z_j\rvert.\]What is the maximum possible value of $\Delta$? Is it maximised by taking the $z_i$ to be the vertices of a regular polygon?
